title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Virginia confronts George about his motive for asking her to pose as a genuine British lady for Canadian visitors. George claims his goal is to influence Mr. McGregor regarding the necessity of a monarchist restoration in Hesse to ensure European peace, specifically proposing Prince Michael O'Paulwich as the king. Virginia dismisses his explanation as a terrible lie, noting that the media is already saturated with rumors regarding the O'Paulwich dynasty and the late Nicolas IV. George, realizing Virginia is more inquisitive and less docile than he anticipated, attempts to withdraw from the arrangement. However, Virginia informs him that she intends to attend a gathering at the Chimney House over the weekend to investigate the "McGrath mystery." After George leaves, Virginia calls Miss Elinor Brent to confirm her attendance at the Chimney House.

Meanwhile, at the Bretton Hotel, Anthony McGlass deals with the disappearance of important letters. He believes a waiter named Giuseppe, possibly working for the "Red Hand League" or Royalists, stole them after mistaking them for a memoir. Anthony meets with the hotel manager to report the incident. Although Anthony initially claims only trivial items were taken to avoid a scene, he eventually admits to the manager that the stolen items are valuable letters. The manager, wishing to avoid a media scandal, agrees not to involve the police. Anthony requests information on Giuseppe, and the manager later provides details, noting that Giuseppe is a skilled waiter who has been in Britain for five years.
